name: ACE | Access to Capital for Entrepreneurs
description_short: Georgia-based CDFI offering small business loans ($15K–$1.5M) with free coaching, advisory services, and networking support to underserved entrepreneurs.
description_long:
ACE (Access to Capital for Entrepreneurs) is a Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI) operating in Georgia since 2000. It has originated over $295 million in loans to more than 2,950 businesses while creating or retaining 24,300+ jobs and delivering 500,000+ consulting hours. The organization was founded to serve small business owners who face barriers accessing traditional lending and to strengthen economic opportunity in vulnerable communities.

ACE provides small business loans ranging from $15,000 to $1.5 million, with offerings including standard small business loans and commercial loans. Unlike traditional lenders, ACE bundles capital with personalized advisory coaching throughout the loan lifecycle. The organization operates a Women's Business Center offering cohort-based training, one-on-one coaching in English and Spanish, business readiness assessments, and client networking through its Client Network Directory. They also operate an Initiate Prosperity program and Connections initiative to build business networks.

ACE distinguishes itself through a holistic support model rather than loan-only products. Their board includes established professionals like William J. Zawrotny (Partner at Jones Day) and Sibyl Slade (founder of IntegriVest Wealth Advisors), reflecting a commitment to quality governance. The organization explicitly targets underserved populations and markets itself as giving "people a chance when others can't," focusing on rural Georgia and minority-owned businesses. They also maintain a veteran loan program.

As a CDFI, ACE prioritizes mission-driven lending over maximum profit, making them accessible to borrowers traditional banks reject. However, loan approval still requires demonstrating business viability, and coaching is provided after loan origination rather than before, meaning applicants must qualify first. The organization's footprint appears limited to Georgia, and specific APR/rate information is not detailed on their public website.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Comprehensive support model: loans bundled with free advisory coaching, business readiness assessment, and networking throughout loan lifecycle
Low minimum loan size ($15K) compared to most traditional banks, accessible to very small businesses
Women's Business Center with bilingual (English/Spanish) one-on-one coaching and cohort-based training
Proven track record since 2000: $295M+ in loans, 2,950+ businesses funded, 24,300+ jobs created/retained
Mission-driven CDFI explicitly serving underserved entrepreneurs, rural Georgia, and minority-owned businesses
Veteran Loan Program specifically for military veterans
Client testimonials and success stories publicly available (Enhanced Estètic, RapiFast, Cycle Gang, etc.)
cons: Geographic limitation: operates only in Georgia; not available to entrepreneurs in other states
No published interest rates or APR information on website; borrowers must apply or contact for pricing details
Coaching provided post-loan, not pre-qualification support; applicants must demonstrate business readiness independently to qualify
Loan calculator and specific eligibility criteria not detailed on public website; opaque qualification process
Limited transparency on approval rates, average loan terms, or typical timeline from application to funding
best_for: Georgia-based women entrepreneurs seeking business loans with ongoing coaching and peer networking support
Small business owners rejected by traditional banks due to limited credit history, collateral, or credit score
Minority-owned businesses and rural Georgia entrepreneurs looking for mission-driven lenders invested in community economic development
Veteran business owners in Georgia seeking dedicated loan programs and advisory services
diagnosis: ACE is best for Georgia-based small business owners—especially women, minorities, and veterans—who have been declined by traditional lenders and want capital paired with ongoing coaching and business connections. The main caveat is geographic limitation to Georgia and the requirement to demonstrate business viability before receiving support, limiting accessibility for absolute beginners.
services: Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) Cohort Series – Metro Atlanta
Business Readiness Assessment and 'How to Get Your Business Loan Ready' resources
Client Networking through Client Network Directory
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
